steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence states an assumption: offshore and land-based farms of similar size produce the same amount of energy. The second sentence contradicts that assumption by explaining offshore turbines actually produce more. The blank must carry a transition word that signals a direct contradiction of what was just assumed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Actually,': "Actually" is a correction marker — it signals that reality differs from a prior assumption. That is exactly the relationship here: the reader assumed equal output, but in fact offshore produces more. ✓
  • Choice B — 'For example,': "For example" introduces evidence that supports the preceding idea. The second sentence contradicts the first, so a supporting connector is wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Therefore,': "Therefore" introduces a logical result or conclusion drawn from what came before. The second sentence is not a consequence of the assumption; it refutes it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Finally,': "Finally" marks the last item in a sequence or a concluding step. There is no sequence here, and the sentence is a correction, not a conclusion. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
